epigraph.text: Ask, and it will be given to you.
epigraph.quote: Conditioning is the soul of statistics.

content.text:
Given information about one variable, how does our knowledge about 
another variable change? Conditional distributions answer this question.

content.title: Conditional PMF
content.content:
The **conditional PMF** of $Y$ given $X = x$ is:
\[
 p_{Y|X}(y \mid x) = P(Y = y \mid X = x) = \frac{p(x, y)}{p_X(x)}
\]
provided $p_X(x) > 0$.

content.title: Conditional PMF
content.problem:
Using the joint PMF from Section 5.1:

| $Y=0$ | $Y=1$ | $Y=2$ |
| $X=0$ | 0.10 | 0.15 | 0.05 |
| $X=1$ | 0.20 | 0.25 | 0.25 |

Find the conditional distribution of $Y$ given $X = 0$.

content.title: Conditional PDF
content.content:
The **conditional PDF** of $Y$ given $X = x$ is:
\[
 f_{Y|X}(y \mid x) = \frac{f(x, y)}{f_X(x)}
\]

content.title: Conditional Expectation
content.content:
The **conditional expectation** 
of $Y$ given $X = x$ is:
\[
 \E[Y \mid X = x] = \sum_y y \cdot p_{Y|X}(y \mid x) \quad \text{(discrete)}
\]
\[
 \E[Y \mid X = x] = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} y \cdot f_{Y|X}(y \mid x) \, dy \quad \text{(continuous)}
\]

content.title: Law of Total Expectation
content.content:
\[
 \E[Y] = \E[\E[Y \mid X]]
\]
That is, the expected value of $Y$ equals the expected value of the 
conditional expectation.
content.label: Theorem
